255	The adapter registry stores parent\_ids (JSON list) and generation
256	(integer) for every adapter, enabling full lineage tracing. Write-once
257	enforcement ensures adapter weights and hashes are immutable---only
258	metadata (fitness, archived status) is mutable.
259	
260	5\. Validation and Kill-Switch Gate
261	
262	5.1 Primary Gate: Pass\@1 Improvement
263	
264	The kill-switch requires ≥5% Pass\@1 improvement on HumanEval over the
265	unmodified base model:
266	
267	-   **Benchmark:** HumanEval, 20--30 held-out tasks, k=5 samples per
268	    task
269	
270	-   **Formula:** Pass\@1 = (1/N) ∑ 1\[task\_i passes on ≥1 of k
271	    samples\]
272	
273	-   **Expected range:** 5--15% improvement signals real effect
274	
275	-   **If gate fails:** Stop and reassess before investing further GPU
276	    time
277	
278	5.2 Baselines
279	
280	  ------------------------------- ------------------------------------------ ---------------------------------
281	  **Condition**                   **Description**                            **Purpose**
282	  Vanilla model                   Qwen3.5-9B base (bfloat16, no adapter)     Lower bound
283	  RAG baseline                    Retrieved trajectory snippets in context   Context-space memory ceiling
284	  QLoRA (DeltaCoder warm-start)   Stage 1 adapter on same trajectory data    Weight-space upper bound
285	  Hypernetwork-generated          Stage 2 single forward pass                Production system quality check
286	  ------------------------------- ------------------------------------------ ---------------------------------
287	
288	5.3 Diagnostic Metrics (Non-Gating)
289	
290	-   **Adapter Frobenius norm \|\|**ΔW**\|\|\_F:** Must be meaningfully
291	    nonzero---confirms adapter modifies model behavior
292	
293	-   **Inter-adapter cosine diversity \> 0.1:** Confirms diversity, not
294	    mode collapse
295	
296	-   **Training loss convergence:** Should decrease without early plateau
297	
298	5.4 PBB-Inspired Procedural Encoding Test
299	
300	Beyond Pass\@1: generate an adapter from a trajectory containing a
301	specific algorithm, load it, and query with held-out inputs *not in the
302	trajectory and not in context*. If the adapter-loaded model executes the
303	procedure better than the base model on novel inputs, the adapter has
304	encoded procedural knowledge in weight space.
305	
306	6\. Inference Configuration
307	
308	6.1 Temperature Recalibration
309	
310	The current optimized temperature of 0.25 was derived on
311	Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct. DeltaCoder's model card documents 0.5 as the
312	hard minimum for multi-turn agentic use. After migration, re-run
313	scripts/optimization/run\_optimization.py (Bayesian optimization, 200+
314	trials) on the new stack.
315	
316	6.2 Sampling Defaults
317	
318	  -------------------- ----------------- ----------- ----------- --------------------------------------
319	  **Context**          **Temperature**   **Top-k**   **Top-p**   **Notes**
320	  Multi-turn agentic   0.6               20          0.95        DeltaCoder model card recommendation
321	  Deterministic/code   0.2--0.4          ---         ---         Lower for consistent output
322	  Exploration phases   0.7--0.8          30          0.95        Higher for diverse decomposition
323	  Repetition penalty   1.04              ---         ---         Bayesian-optimized on previous stack
324	  -------------------- ----------------- ----------- ----------- --------------------------------------
325	
326	6.3 Serving Architecture
327	
328	vLLM with S-LoRA unified paging enables serving with up to 8 concurrent
329	adapters. Single-GPU training coordinates via sleep/wake endpoints.
330	Multi-GPU uses pipeline parallelism (not tensor parallelism---vLLM issue
331	\#21471 documents LoRA corruption on PCIe without NVLink).
332	
333	7\. Execution Plan
334	
335	7.1 Phase 0: Environment Validation (1--2 days)
336	
337	-   Load Qwen3.5-9B in vLLM with LoRA enabled; confirm GDN support
338	
339	-   Verify AWQ/GPTQ quantization availability
340	
341	-   Load DeltaCoder via PeftModel.from\_pretrained; confirm no shape
342	    errors
343	
344	-   Print state\_dict keys to confirm GDN module paths empirically
345	
346	-   Run HumanEval baseline (bare model, no adapter)
347	
348	7.2 Phase 1: Code Changes + Stage 1 Corpus (3--5 days)
349	
350	-   Implement code changes from Report 1 (hypernetwork, d2l\_config,
351	    probe, sakana\_d2l, serving)
352	
353	-   Warm-start from DeltaCoder; fine-tune on 50--100 diverse coding
354	    trajectories
355	
356	-   Evaluate Pass\@1 on held-out tasks; apply kill-switch gate (≥5%
357	    improvement or stop)
358	
359	7.3 Phase 2: Hypernetwork Training (2--4 GPU-days)
360	
361	-   Transfer Sakana qwen\_4b\_d2l aggregator; freeze aggregator, retrain
362	    head on Stage 1 corpus
363	
364	-   Validate hypernetwork-generated adapters close the gap to QLoRA

file.content:
The adapter registry stores parent\_ids (JSON list) and generation
(integer) for every adapter, enabling full lineage tracing. Write-once
enforcement ensures adapter weights and hashes are immutable---only
metadata (fitness, archived status) is mutable.

5\. Validation and Kill-Switch Gate

5.1 Primary Gate: Pass\@1 Improvement

The kill-switch requires ≥5% Pass\@1 improvement on HumanEval over the
unmodified base model:

-   **Benchmark:** HumanEval, 20--30 held-out tasks, k=5 samples per
    task

-   **Formula:** Pass\@1 = (1/N) ∑ 1\[task\_i passes on ≥1 of k
    samples\]

-   **Expected range:** 5--15% improvement signals real effect

-   **If gate fails:** Stop and reassess before investing further GPU
    time

5.2 Baselines

  ------------------------------- ------------------------------------------ ---------------------------------
  **Condition**                   **Description**                            **Purpose**
  Vanilla model                   Qwen3.5-9B base (bfloat16, no adapter)     Lower bound
  RAG baseline                    Retrieved trajectory snippets in context   Context-space memory ceiling
  QLoRA (DeltaCoder warm-start)   Stage 1 adapter on same trajectory data    Weight-space upper bound
  Hypernetwork-generated          Stage 2 single forward pass                Production system quality check
  ------------------------------- ------------------------------------------ ---------------------------------

5.3 Diagnostic Metrics (Non-Gating)

-   **Adapter Frobenius norm \|\|**ΔW**\|\|\_F:** Must be meaningfully
    nonzero---confirms adapter modifies model behavior

-   **Inter-adapter cosine diversity \> 0.1:** Confirms diversity, not
    mode collapse

-   **Training loss convergence:** Should decrease without early plateau

5.4 PBB-Inspired Procedural Encoding Test

Beyond Pass\@1: generate an adapter from a trajectory containing a
specific algorithm, load it, and query with held-out inputs *not in the
trajectory and not in context*. If the adapter-loaded model executes the
procedure better than the base model on novel inputs, the adapter has
encoded procedural knowledge in weight space.

6\. Inference Configuration

6.1 Temperature Recalibration

The current optimized temperature of 0.25 was derived on
Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct. DeltaCoder's model card documents 0.5 as the
hard minimum for multi-turn agentic use. After migration, re-run
scripts/optimization/run\_optimization.py (Bayesian optimization, 200+
trials) on the new stack.

6.2 Sampling Defaults

  -------------------- ----------------- ----------- ----------- --------------------------------------
  **Context**          **Temperature**   **Top-k**   **Top-p**   **Notes**
  Multi-turn agentic   0.6               20          0.95        DeltaCoder model card recommendation
  Deterministic/code   0.2--0.4          ---         ---         Lower for consistent output
  Exploration phases   0.7--0.8          30          0.95        Higher for diverse decomposition
  Repetition penalty   1.04              ---         ---         Bayesian-optimized on previous stack
  -------------------- ----------------- ----------- ----------- --------------------------------------

6.3 Serving Architecture

vLLM with S-LoRA unified paging enables serving with up to 8 concurrent
adapters. Single-GPU training coordinates via sleep/wake endpoints.
Multi-GPU uses pipeline parallelism (not tensor parallelism---vLLM issue
\#21471 documents LoRA corruption on PCIe without NVLink).

7\. Execution Plan

7.1 Phase 0: Environment Validation (1--2 days)

-   Load Qwen3.5-9B in vLLM with LoRA enabled; confirm GDN support

-   Verify AWQ/GPTQ quantization availability

-   Load DeltaCoder via PeftModel.from\_pretrained; confirm no shape
    errors

-   Print state\_dict keys to confirm GDN module paths empirically

-   Run HumanEval baseline (bare model, no adapter)

7.2 Phase 1: Code Changes + Stage 1 Corpus (3--5 days)

-   Implement code changes from Report 1 (hypernetwork, d2l\_config,
    probe, sakana\_d2l, serving)

-   Warm-start from DeltaCoder; fine-tune on 50--100 diverse coding
    trajectories

-   Evaluate Pass\@1 on held-out tasks; apply kill-switch gate (≥5%
    improvement or stop)

7.3 Phase 2: Hypernetwork Training (2--4 GPU-days)

-   Transfer Sakana qwen\_4b\_d2l aggregator; freeze aggregator, retrain
    head on Stage 1 corpus

-   Validate hypernetwork-generated adapters close the gap to QLoRA

content.content:
1	"""KL-divergence context distillation training loop for Qwen3-Coder-Next.
2	
3	Assembles all Phase 25-28 components (config, data pipeline, activation
4	extraction, weight transfer, functional LoRA injection) into a complete
5	distillation training script.
6	
7	Three execution modes:
8	- dry-run: loads real base model + hypernet, validates tensor shapes, exits
9	- smoke-test: 5 training steps, verifies finite loss and decreasing trend
10	- full: trains from JSONL dataset with tiered checkpointing and MLflow tracking
11	
12	All heavy GPU imports (torch, transformers, peft) are deferred to function
13	bodies per INFRA-05 project convention.
14	
15	Usage:
16	    uv run python -m model_training.d2l_train --dry-run
17	    uv run python -m model_training.d2l_train --smoke-test
18	    uv run python -m model_training.d2l_train --dataset path/to/train.jsonl
19	"""
20	
21	from __future__ import annotations
22	
23	import logging
24	import os
25	from pathlib import Path
26	from typing import Any
27	
28	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator
29	
30	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
31	
32	__all__ = ["train_d2l_qwen3", "D2LTrainConfig", "compute_kl_ce_loss"]
33	
34	
35	def _require_probe_cache(model_name: str) -> None:
36	    """Raise RuntimeError if probe cache is absent for *model_name*.
37	
38	    Called before build_qwen3_hypernet_config so training never proceeds with
39	    placeholder feature_sizes that produce incorrect LoRA dimensions.
40	
41	    Defers the import of load_probe_cache so GPU-free test environments are
42	    not forced to import the full probe module at module load time.
43	
44	    Args:
45	        model_name: Canonical model identifier for cache lookup and error message.
46	
47	    Raises:
48	        RuntimeError: If no probe cache is found for model_name.
49	    """
50	    from model_training.d2l_probe import load_probe_cache  # noqa: PLC0415
51	
52	    if load_probe_cache(model_name) is not None:
53	        return
54	    msg = (
55	        f"Probe cache not found for '{model_name}' — "
56	        "run probe_model() and save_probe_cache() before training. "
57	        "Training with placeholder feature_sizes produces incorrect LoRA "
58	        "dimensions."
59	    )
60	    raise RuntimeError(msg)
61	
62	
63	class D2LTrainConfig(BaseModel):
64	    """Pydantic model for D2L training hyperparameters.
65	
66	    Enables validation, JSON serialization (for checkpoint storage), and
67	    `.model_dump()` for MLflow experiment logging.
68	
69	    Attributes:
70	        model_config_name: Registry lookup key (e.g. "qwen3.5-9b"). Used to
71	            resolve base_model_name from the model registry when not set.
72	        base_model_name: HuggingFace model name for the student/teacher base.
73	        sakana_checkpoint_path: Path to the Sakana hypernet checkpoint.
74	        num_steps: Total training steps.
75	        lr: Learning rate for AdamW optimizer.
76	        alpha: Blending weight for KL vs CE loss (1.0 = pure KL, 0.0 = pure CE).
77	        temperature: Softmax temperature for KL divergence computation.
78	        checkpoint_every: Steps between lightweight checkpoint saves.
79	        full_checkpoint_every: Steps between full checkpoint saves (incl. optimizer).
80	        checkpoint_dir: Directory for checkpoint output.
81	        experiment_name: MLflow experiment name.
82	        dry_run: If True, validate tensor shapes then exit.
83	        smoke_test: If True, run 5 steps and verify loss trend.
84	        dataset_path: Path to training JSONL file (required for full training).
85	        grad_clip: Gradient clipping max norm.
86	        warmup_steps: Number of linear LR warmup steps.
87	        lora_r: LoRA rank.
88	        max_length: Maximum tokenizer sequence length.
89	    """
90	
91	    model_config_name: str = Field(default="qwen3.5-9b")
92	    base_model_name: str = Field(default="")
93	    sakana_checkpoint_path: str
94	    num_steps: int = Field(default=100)
95	    lr: float = Field(default=2e-4)
96	    alpha: float = Field(default=0.5)
97	    temperature: float = Field(default=2.0)
98	    checkpoint_every: int = Field(default=100)
99	    full_checkpoint_every: int = Field(default=500)
100	    checkpoint_dir: str = Field(default="./checkpoints")
101	    experiment_name: str = Field(default="d2l-qwen3")
102	    dry_run: bool = Field(default=False)
103	    smoke_test: bool = Field(default=False)
104	    dataset_path: str | None = Field(default=None)
105	    grad_clip: float = Field(default=1.0)
106	    warmup_steps: int = Field(default=10)
107	    lora_r: int = Field(default=8)
108	    max_length: int = Field(default=512)
109	
110	    def model_post_init(self, __context: Any) -> None:
111	        """Resolve base_model_name from registry when not explicitly set."""
112	        if not self.base_model_name:
113	            from model_training.model_configs import ModelRegistry
114	
115	            mc = ModelRegistry.default().get(self.model_config_name)
116	            object.__setattr__(self, "base_model_name", mc.model_id)
117	
118	    @field_validator("lr")
119	    @classmethod
120	    def _validate_lr(cls, v: float) -> float:
121	        """Ensure learning rate is strictly positive."""
122	        if v <= 0:
123	            raise ValueError(f"lr must be > 0, got {v}")
124	        return v
125	
126	    @field_validator("alpha")
127	    @classmethod
128	    def _validate_alpha(cls, v: float) -> float:
129	        """Ensure alpha is in [0, 1]."""
130	        if not (0.0 <= v <= 1.0):
131	            raise ValueError(f"alpha must be in [0, 1], got {v}")
132	        return v
133	
134	    @field_validator("temperature")
135	    @classmethod
136	    def _validate_temperature(cls, v: float) -> float:
137	        """Ensure temperature is strictly positive."""
138	        if v <= 0:
139	            raise ValueError(f"temperature must be > 0, got {v}")
140	        return v
141	
142	    @field_validator("num_steps")
143	    @classmethod
144	    def _validate_num_steps(cls, v: int) -> int:
145	        """Ensure num_steps is strictly positive."""
146	        if v <= 0:
147	            raise ValueError(f"num_steps must be > 0, got {v}")
148	        return v

150	
151	def _compute_kl_ce_loss(
152	    student_logits: Any,
153	    teacher_logits: Any,
154	    answer_start: int,
155	    config: D2LTrainConfig,
156	) -> tuple[Any, dict[str, float]]:
157	    """Compute blended KL-divergence and cross-entropy loss over the answer span.
158	
159	    Accounts for the causal LM shift: logits at position ``k`` predict token
160	    ``k+1``, so we slice starting at ``answer_start - 1`` (clamped to 0).
161	    Returns a zero-loss tensor when the answer span is empty (e.g. truncation
162	    pushed ``answer_start`` beyond the sequence length).
163	
164	    Args:
165	        student_logits: Student model logits of shape (batch, seq_len, vocab).
166	        teacher_logits: Teacher model logits of shape (batch, seq_len, vocab).
167	        answer_start: Token index where the answer span begins.  Tokens before
168	            this index are excluded from loss computation.
169	        config: Training configuration supplying alpha and temperature.
170	
171	    Returns:
172	        A tuple of:
173	        - total_loss: Blended scalar tensor ``alpha * kl + (1 - alpha) * ce``.
174	        - metrics: Dict with keys ``kl_loss``, ``ce_loss``, ``total_loss``
175	          (Python floats, suitable for MLflow logging).
176	    """
177	    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
178	    import torch.nn.functional as functional  # noqa: PLC0415
179	
180	    alpha = config.alpha
181	    temp = config.temperature
182	
183	    # Guard against empty answer span (truncation pushed answer beyond seq_len).
184	    # answer_start >= seq_len means no answer tokens exist in the sequence.
185	    seq_len = student_logits.shape[1]
186	    if answer_start >= seq_len:
187	        logger.warning(
188	            "Answer span empty: answer_start=%d >= seq_len=%d. Returning zero loss.",
189	            answer_start,
190	            seq_len,
191	        )
192	        zero = torch.tensor(0.0, device=student_logits.device, requires_grad=True)
193	        return zero, {"kl_loss": 0.0, "ce_loss": 0.0, "total_loss": 0.0}
194	
195	    # Causal LM shift: logits at position k predict token k+1, so the logit
196	    # that predicts the first answer token is at position answer_start - 1.
197	    logit_start = max(0, answer_start - 1)
198	
199	    # Slice to answer span only (shift-aware)
200	    s = student_logits[:, logit_start:, :]
201	    t = teacher_logits[:, logit_start:, :]
202	
203	    vocab = s.shape[-1]
204	
205	    # KL divergence: temperature-scaled, batchmean reduction
206	    kl = functional.kl_div(
207	        functional.log_softmax(s / temp, dim=-1),
208	        functional.softmax(t / temp, dim=-1),
209	        reduction="batchmean",
210	    )
211	
212	    # CE: student predictions vs teacher hard labels
213	    ce = functional.cross_entropy(s.reshape(-1, vocab), t.argmax(-1).reshape(-1))
214	
215	    # Blended loss
216	    total = alpha * kl + (1 - alpha) * ce
217	
218	    return total, {
219	        "kl_loss": kl.item(),
220	        "ce_loss": ce.item(),
221	        "total_loss": total.item(),
222	    }
223	
224	
225	def _training_step(
226	    record: dict[str, Any],
227	    base_model: Any,
228	    tokenizer: Any,
229	    hypernet: Any,
230	    hc: Any,
231	    config: D2LTrainConfig,
232	) -> tuple[Any, dict[str, float]]:
233	    """Execute a single training step with two-pass teacher/student separation.
234	
235	    Pass 1 extracts activations from activation_text only (no answer tokens).
236	    Pass 2 runs teacher (no_grad) and student (with LoRA) on teacher_text.
237	
238	    Args:
239	        record: Data record with 'activation_text' and 'teacher_text' fields.
240	        base_model: Base LM in eval mode.
241	        tokenizer: Tokenizer matching base_model.
242	        hypernet: HyperLoRA in train mode.
243	        hc: HypernetConfig with layer_indices and lora_config.
244	        config: Training configuration.
245	
246	    Returns:
247	        Tuple of (loss_tensor, metrics_dict).
248	    """
249	    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
250	
251	    from model_training.d2l_lora import apply_functional_lora  # noqa: PLC0415
252	    from model_training.d2l_probe import extract_activations_with_model  # noqa: PLC0415
253	
254	    # Pass 1: extract activations from activation_text (context only, no answer tokens)
255	    features, attn_mask = extract_activations_with_model(
256	        text=record["activation_text"],
257	        model=base_model,
258	        tokenizer=tokenizer,
259	        layer_indices=list(hc.layer_indices),
260	        max_length=config.max_length,
261	    )
262	
263	    # Hypernetwork forward — OUTSIDE torch.no_grad to preserve autograd graph
264	    lora_dict, _ = hypernet.generate_weights(features, attn_mask, None)
265	
266	    # Compute answer_start: token offset where answer begins in teacher_text
267	    answer_start = len(
268	        tokenizer(
269	            record["activation_text"],
270	            truncation=True,
271	            max_length=config.max_length,
272	        )["input_ids"]
273	    )
274	
275	    # Pass 2 teacher: run base model under no_grad to get teacher logits
276	    teacher_inputs = tokenizer(
277	        record["teacher_text"],
278	        return_tensors="pt",
279	        truncation=True,
280	        max_length=config.max_length,
281	    )
282	    try:
283	        device = next(base_model.parameters()).device
284	    except StopIteration:
285	        device = torch.device("cpu")
286	    teacher_inputs = {k: v.to(device) for k, v in teacher_inputs.items()}
287	
288	    with torch.no_grad():
289	        teacher_out = base_model(**teacher_inputs, output_hidden_states=False)
290	    teacher_logits = teacher_out.logits
291	
292	    # Pass 2 student: run base model with functional LoRA patches
293	    with apply_functional_lora(base_model, lora_dict, hc):
294	        student_out = base_model(**teacher_inputs, output_hidden_states=False)
295	    student_logits = student_out.logits
296	
297	    return _compute_kl_ce_loss(student_logits, teacher_logits, answer_start, config)
298	
299	
300	def _save_checkpoint(
301	    step: int,
302	    hypernet: Any,
303	    optimizer: Any,
304	    scheduler: Any,
305	    config: D2LTrainConfig,
306	    hc: Any,
307	    best_loss: float,
308	    full: bool = False,
309	) -> Path:
310	    """Save a tiered checkpoint to disk.
311	
312	    Lightweight checkpoint: hypernet weights, config, step, layer indices, best_loss.
313	    Full checkpoint: additionally includes optimizer state, scheduler state, RNG state.
314	
315	    Args:
316	        step: Current training step number.
317	        hypernet: HyperLoRA model.
318	        optimizer: AdamW optimizer.
319	        scheduler: LR scheduler.
320	        config: Training configuration.
321	        hc: HypernetConfig with attention_layer_indices.
322	        best_loss: Best loss seen so far.
323	        full: If True, save full checkpoint with optimizer/scheduler/RNG state.
324	
325	    Returns:
326	        Path to the saved checkpoint file.
327	    """
328	    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
329	
330	    ckpt_dir = Path(config.checkpoint_dir)
331	    ckpt_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
332	
333	    payload: dict[str, Any] = {
334	        "hypernet_state_dict": hypernet.state_dict(),
335	        "config_json": config.model_dump(),
336	        "step": step,
337	        "attention_layer_indices": list(hc.layer_indices),
338	        "best_loss": best_loss,
339	    }
340	
341	    if full:
342	        payload["optimizer_state_dict"] = optimizer.state_dict()
343	        payload["scheduler_state_dict"] = scheduler.state_dict()
344	        payload["rng_state"] = torch.get_rng_state()
345	        if torch.cuda.is_available():
346	            payload["cuda_rng_state"] = torch.cuda.get_rng_state()
347	
348	    ckpt_path = ckpt_dir / f"ckpt-{step}.pt"
349	    torch.save(payload, ckpt_path)
350	    logger.info("Checkpoint saved: %s (full=%s)", ckpt_path, full)
351	    return ckpt_path
352

355	    """Configure MLflow tracking URI and experiment name.
356	
357	    Delegates to training_common.setup_mlflow. The d2l training loop always
358	    calls mlflow.start_run() directly after this, so we do not gate on
359	    RUNE_DISABLE_MLFLOW here — that gate lives in setup_mlflow itself but the
360	    existing callers in train_d2l_qwen3 call mlflow directly after this
361	    function returns, so we preserve the unconditional call behaviour.
362	
363	    Args:
364	        config: Training configuration supplying experiment_name.
365	    """
366	    from model_training.training_common import setup_mlflow  # noqa: PLC0415
367	
368	    setup_mlflow(
369	        config.experiment_name,
370	        os.environ.get("MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI"),
371	    )

374	def _dry_run_validate_shapes(config: D2LTrainConfig) -> dict[str, Any]:
375	    """Validate tensor shapes with a single forward pass, no optimizer step.
376	
377	    Loads the base model and hypernet, generates one needle record, runs one
378	    training step without backward/optimizer, asserts all shapes are correct,
379	    and returns a shape summary.
380	
381	    Args:
382	        config: Training configuration.
383	
384	    Returns:
385	        Dict with shape validation results: features, lora_dict, student/teacher logits.
386	    """
387	    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
388	    from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer  # noqa: PLC0415
389	
390	    from model_training.d2l_config import build_hypernet_config  # noqa: PLC0415
391	    from model_training.d2l_data import generate_needle_dataset  # noqa: PLC0415
392	    from model_training.d2l_probe import (  # noqa: PLC0415
393	        extract_activations_with_model,
394	    )
395	    from model_training.sakana_d2l import (  # noqa: PLC0415
396	        get_aggregator_config,
397	        transfer_aggregator_weights,
398	    )
399	
400	    # Guard: dry-run also requires probe cache to produce correct LoRA dimensions.
401	    _require_probe_cache(config.model_config_name)
402	
403	    logger.info("Dry-run: loading tokenizer and base model...")
404	    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(config.base_model_name)
405	    base_model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(
406	        config.base_model_name,
407	        output_hidden_states=True,
408	    ).eval()
409	
410	    logger.info("Dry-run: building hypernet config and transferring weights...")
411	    hc = build_hypernet_config(
412	        config.model_config_name,
413	        aggregator_config=get_aggregator_config(config.sakana_checkpoint_path),
414	        lora_r=config.lora_r,
415	    )
416	
417	    from ctx_to_lora.modeling.hypernet import HyperLoRA  # noqa: PLC0415
418	
419	    hypernet = HyperLoRA(hc).to(torch.float32)
420	    hypernet = transfer_aggregator_weights(hypernet, config.sakana_checkpoint_path)
421	    hypernet.eval()
422	
423	    device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
424	    base_model = base_model.to(device)
425	    hypernet = hypernet.to(device)
426	
427	    records = generate_needle_dataset(n=1)
428	    record = records[0]
429	
430	    logger.info("Dry-run: running single forward pass for shape validation...")
431	    features, attn_mask = extract_activations_with_model(
432	        text=record["activation_text"],
433	        model=base_model,
434	        tokenizer=tokenizer,
435	        layer_indices=list(hc.layer_indices),
436	        max_length=config.max_length,
437	    )
438	
439	    with torch.no_grad():
440	        lora_dict, _ = hypernet.generate_weights(features, attn_mask, None)
441	
442	    tokenizer_out = tokenizer(
443	        record["teacher_text"],
444	        return_tensors="pt",
445	        truncation=True,
446	        max_length=config.max_length,
447	    )
448	    teacher_inputs = {k: v.to(device) for k, v in tokenizer_out.items()}
449	
450	    with torch.no_grad():
451	        teacher_out = base_model(**teacher_inputs, output_hidden_states=False)
452	    teacher_logits = teacher_out.logits
453	
454	    from model_training.d2l_lora import apply_functional_lora  # noqa: PLC0415
455	
456	    with apply_functional_lora(base_model, lora_dict, hc):
457	        with torch.no_grad():
458	            student_out = base_model(**teacher_inputs, output_hidden_states=False)
459	    student_logits = student_out.logits
460	
461	    # Assert shapes
462	    assert features.ndim == 4, f"features must be 4D, got {features.ndim}D"  # noqa: S101
463	    assert student_logits.shape == teacher_logits.shape, (  # noqa: S101
464	        f"student/teacher logit shapes must match: "
465	        f"{student_logits.shape} vs {teacher_logits.shape}"
466	    )
467	
468	    shape_summary = {
469	        "features_shape": list(features.shape),
470	        "attn_mask_shape": list(attn_mask.shape),
471	        "lora_modules": list(lora_dict.keys()),
472	        "student_logits_shape": list(student_logits.shape),
473	        "teacher_logits_shape": list(teacher_logits.shape),
474	        "status": "OK",
475	    }
476	
477	    for mod_name, ab in lora_dict.items():
478	        shape_summary[f"lora_{mod_name}_A_shape"] = list(ab["A"].shape)
479	        shape_summary[f"lora_{mod_name}_B_shape"] = list(ab["B"].shape)
480	
481	    logger.info("Dry-run shape validation passed: %s", shape_summary)
482	    return shape_summary
483

485	def train_d2l_qwen3(config: D2LTrainConfig) -> dict[str, Any]:  # noqa: C901
486	    """Run KL-divergence context distillation training.
487	
488	    Three execution modes controlled by config flags:
489	    - dry_run=True: Validate shapes with single forward pass, no optimizer step.
490	    - smoke_test=True: Run min(num_steps, 5) steps, assert finite decreasing loss.
491	    - default: Full training from dataset with checkpointing and MLflow tracking.
492	
493	    Args:
494	        config: Training configuration.
495	
496	    Returns:
497	        Dictionary with training results:
498	            - final_loss: Loss at the last step.
499	            - best_loss: Lowest loss seen during training.
500	            - num_steps_completed: Number of training steps completed.
501	            - checkpoint_dir: Path to checkpoint directory.
502	            - shape_summary (dry_run only): Tensor shape validation results.
503	    """
504	    import mlflow  # noqa: PLC0415
505	    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
506	    from ctx_to_lora.modeling.hypernet import HyperLoRA  # noqa: PLC0415
507	    from torch.nn.utils import clip_grad_norm_  # noqa: PLC0415
508	    from torch.optim import AdamW  # noqa: PLC0415
509	    from torch.optim.lr_scheduler import (  # noqa: PLC0415
510	        CosineAnnealingLR,
511	        LinearLR,
512	        SequentialLR,
513	    )
514	    from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer  # noqa: PLC0415
515	
516	    from model_training.d2l_config import build_hypernet_config  # noqa: PLC0415
517	    from model_training.d2l_data import (  # noqa: PLC0415
518	        generate_needle_dataset,
519	        load_jsonl,
520	        split_by_task_id,
521	    )
522	    from model_training.sakana_d2l import (  # noqa: PLC0415
523	        get_aggregator_config,
524	        transfer_aggregator_weights,
525	    )
526	
527	    # Mode dispatch: dry_run exits after shape validation
528	    if config.dry_run:
529	        shape_summary = _dry_run_validate_shapes(config)
530	        return {"shape_summary": shape_summary, "status": "dry_run_complete"}
531	
532	    # Smoke test caps steps at 5
533	    if config.smoke_test:
534	        config = config.model_copy(update={"num_steps": min(config.num_steps, 5)})
535	
536	    # Guard: require probe cache when not in smoke_test mode.
537	    # smoke_test uses generate_needle_dataset and may not have a real probe cache.
538	    if not config.smoke_test:
539	        _require_probe_cache(config.model_config_name)
540	
541	    num_steps = config.num_steps
542	    warmup_steps = config.warmup_steps
543	
544	    # Load tokenizer and base model
545	    logger.info("Loading tokenizer: %s", config.base_model_name)
546	    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(config.base_model_name)
547	
548	    logger.info("Loading base model: %s", config.base_model_name)
549	    base_model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(
550	        config.base_model_name,
551	        output_hidden_states=True,
552	    ).eval()
553	
554	    # Build hypernet config with aggregator config from checkpoint
555	    logger.info(
556	        "Building hypernet config from checkpoint: %s",
557	        config.sakana_checkpoint_path,
558	    )
559	    hc = build_hypernet_config(
560	        config.model_config_name,
561	        aggregator_config=get_aggregator_config(config.sakana_checkpoint_path),
562	        lora_r=config.lora_r,
563	    )
564	
565	    # Create hypernet and transfer aggregator weights (freezes aggregator)
566	    hypernet = HyperLoRA(hc).to(torch.float32)
567	    hypernet = transfer_aggregator_weights(hypernet, config.sakana_checkpoint_path)
568	    hypernet.train()
569	
570	    # Device selection: cuda > mps > cpu
571	    from shared.hardware import get_best_device  # noqa: PLC0415
572	
573	    device = torch.device(get_best_device())
574	    logger.info("Using device: %s", device)
575	    base_model = base_model.to(device)
576	    hypernet = hypernet.to(device)
577	
578	    # Optimizer: only trainable params (head + projections, not frozen aggregator)
579	    trainable_params = [p for p in hypernet.parameters() if p.requires_grad]
580	    logger.info("Trainable params: %d", sum(p.numel() for p in trainable_params))
581	    optimizer = AdamW(trainable_params, lr=config.lr)
582	
583	    # Scheduler: linear warmup → cosine annealing
584	    scheduler = SequentialLR(
585	        optimizer,
586	        schedulers=[
587	            LinearLR(optimizer, start_factor=0.01, total_iters=warmup_steps),
588	            CosineAnnealingLR(
589	                optimizer,
590	                T_max=max(1, num_steps - warmup_steps),
591	                eta_min=1e-6,
592	            ),
593	        ],
594	        milestones=[warmup_steps],
595	    )
596	
597	    # Data loading
598	    if config.smoke_test or config.dataset_path is None:
599	        records = generate_needle_dataset(n=20)
600	        logger.info("Using needle dataset (%d records)", len(records))
601	    else:
602	        all_records = load_jsonl(config.dataset_path)
603	        records, _ = split_by_task_id(all_records)
604	        logger.info(
605	            "Loaded %d training records from %s",
606	            len(records),
607	            config.dataset_path,
608	        )
609	
610	    if not records:
611	        raise ValueError("No training records loaded; cannot train on empty dataset.")
612	
613	    # MLflow setup and training loop
614	    _setup_mlflow(config)
615	
616	    best_loss = float("inf")
617	    final_loss = float("inf")
618	    step_losses: list[float] = []
619	
620	    with mlflow.start_run(run_name=f"{config.experiment_name}-step{num_steps}"):
621	        mlflow.log_params(config.model_dump())
622	
623	        for step in range(1, num_steps + 1):
624	            record = records[(step - 1) % len(records)]
625	
626	            loss, metrics = _training_step(
627	                record=record,
628	                base_model=base_model,
629	                tokenizer=tokenizer,
630	                hypernet=hypernet,
631	                hc=hc,
632	                config=config,
633	            )
634	
635	            loss.backward()
636	            clip_grad_norm_(trainable_params, config.grad_clip)
637	            optimizer.step()
638	            scheduler.step()
639	            optimizer.zero_grad()
640	
641	            step_loss = metrics["total_loss"]
642	            step_losses.append(step_loss)
643	            final_loss = step_loss
644	            if step_loss < best_loss:
645	                best_loss = step_loss
646	
647	            mlflow.log_metrics(metrics, step=step)
648	            logger.info(
649	                "Step %d/%d — loss=%.4f (kl=%.4f, ce=%.4f)",
650	                step,
651	                num_steps,
652	                metrics["total_loss"],
653	                metrics["kl_loss"],
654	                metrics["ce_loss"],
655	            )
656	
657	            # Tiered checkpointing
658	            if step % config.full_checkpoint_every == 0:
659	                ckpt_path = _save_checkpoint(
660	                    step=step,
661	                    hypernet=hypernet,
662	                    optimizer=optimizer,
663	                    scheduler=scheduler,
664	                    config=config,
665	                    hc=hc,
666	                    best_loss=best_loss,
667	                    full=True,
668	                )
669	                mlflow.log_artifact(str(ckpt_path))
670	            elif step % config.checkpoint_every == 0:
671	                ckpt_path = _save_checkpoint(
672	                    step=step,
673	                    hypernet=hypernet,
674	                    optimizer=optimizer,
675	                    scheduler=scheduler,
676	                    config=config,
677	                    hc=hc,
678	                    best_loss=best_loss,
679	                    full=False,
680	                )
681	                mlflow.log_artifact(str(ckpt_path))
682	
683	    # Smoke test assertions
684	    if config.smoke_test:
685	        for i, sl in enumerate(step_losses):
686	            assert torch.isfinite(torch.tensor(sl)), (  # noqa: S101
687	                f"Smoke test: loss at step {i + 1} is not finite: {sl}"
688	            )
689	        assert step_losses[-1] < step_losses[0], (  # noqa: S101
690	            f"Smoke test: final loss {step_losses[-1]:.4f} not less than "
691	            f"initial loss {step_losses[0]:.4f}"
692	        )
693	        assert any(p.grad is not None for p in trainable_params), (  # noqa: S101
694	            "Smoke test: no trainable param has non-None gradient after training"
695	        )
696	
697	    return {
698	        "final_loss": final_loss,
699	        "best_loss": best_loss,
700	        "num_steps_completed": num_steps,
701	        "checkpoint_dir": config.checkpoint_dir,
702	    }
703	
704	
705	# Public alias so callers don't need to reference the private name.
706	compute_kl_ce_loss = _compute_kl_ce_loss
707	
708	
709	if __name__ == "__main__":
710	    import argparse  # noqa: PLC0415
711	
712	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="D2L distillation training")
713	    parser.add_argument("--model-config-name", default="qwen3.5-9b")
714	    parser.add_argument("--base-model-name", default="")
715	    parser.add_argument("--sakana-checkpoint-path", required=True)
716	    parser.add_argument("--num-steps", type=int, default=100)
717	    parser.add_argument("--lr", type=float, default=2e-4)
718	    parser.add_argument("--alpha", type=float, default=0.5)
719	    parser.add_argument("--temperature", type=float, default=2.0)
720	    parser.add_argument("--checkpoint-every", type=int, default=100)

722	    parser.add_argument("--checkpoint-dir", default="./checkpoints")

"""KL-divergence context distillation training loop for Qwen3-Coder-Next.

Assembles all Phase 25-28 components (config, data pipeline, activation
extraction, weight transfer, functional LoRA injection) into a complete
distillation training script.

Three execution modes:
- dry-run: loads real base model + hypernet, validates tensor shapes, exits
- smoke-test: 5 training steps, verifies finite loss and decreasing trend
- full: trains from JSONL dataset with tiered checkpointing and MLflow tracking

All heavy GPU imports (torch, transformers, peft) are deferred to function
bodies per INFRA-05 project convention.

Usage:
    uv run python -m model_training.d2l_train --dry-run
    uv run python -m model_training.d2l_train --smoke-test
    uv run python -m model_training.d2l_train --dataset path/to/train.jsonl
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import os
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

__all__ = ["train_d2l_qwen3", "D2LTrainConfig", "compute_kl_ce_loss"]


def _require_probe_cache(model_name: str) -> None:
    """Raise RuntimeError if probe cache is absent for *model_name*.

    Called before build_qwen3_hypernet_config so training never proceeds with
    placeholder feature_sizes that produce incorrect LoRA dimensions.

    Defers the import of load_probe_cache so GPU-free test environments are
    not forced to import the full probe module at module load time.

    Args:
        model_name: Canonical model identifier for cache lookup and error message.

    Raises:
        RuntimeError: If no probe cache is found for model_name.
    """
    from model_training.d2l_probe import load_probe_cache  # noqa: PLC0415

    if load_probe_cache(model_name) is not None:
        return
    msg = (
        f"Probe cache not found for '{model_name}' — "
        "run probe_model() and save_probe_cache() before training. "
        "Training with placeholder feature_sizes produces incorrect LoRA "
        "dimensions."
    )
    raise RuntimeError(msg)


class D2LTrainConfig(BaseModel):
    """Pydantic model for D2L training hyperparameters.

    Enables validation, JSON serialization (for checkpoint storage), and
    `.model_dump()` for MLflow experiment logging.

    Attributes:
        model_config_name: Registry lookup key (e.g. "qwen3.5-9b"). Used to
            resolve base_model_name from the model registry when not set.
        base_model_name: HuggingFace model name for the student/teacher base.
        sakana_checkpoint_path: Path to the Sakana hypernet checkpoint.
        num_steps: Total training steps.
        lr: Learning rate for AdamW optimizer.
        alpha: Blending weight for KL vs CE loss (1.0 = pure KL, 0.0 = pure CE).
        temperature: Softmax temperature for KL divergence computation.
        checkpoint_every: Steps between lightweight checkpoint saves.
        full_checkpoint_every: Steps between full checkpoint saves (incl. optimizer).
        checkpoint_dir: Directory for checkpoint output.
        experiment_name: MLflow experiment name.
        dry_run: If True, validate tensor shapes then exit.
        smoke_test: If True, run 5 steps and verify loss trend.
        dataset_path: Path to training JSONL file (required for full training).
        grad_clip: Gradient clipping max norm.
        warmup_steps: Number of linear LR warmup steps.
        lora_r: LoRA rank.
        max_length: Maximum tokenizer sequence length.
    """

    model_config_name: str = Field(default="qwen3.5-9b")
    base_model_name: str = Field(default="")
    sakana_checkpoint_path: str
    num_steps: int = Field(default=100)
    lr: float = Field(default=2e-4)
    alpha: float = Field(default=0.5)
    temperature: float = Field(default=2.0)
    checkpoint_every: int = Field(default=100)
    full_checkpoint_every: int = Field(default=500)
    checkpoint_dir: str = Field(default="./checkpoints")
    experiment_name: str = Field(default="d2l-qwen3")
    dry_run: bool = Field(default=False)
    smoke_test: bool = Field(default=False)
    dataset_path: str | None = Field(default=None)
    grad_clip: float = Field(default=1.0)
    warmup_steps: int = Field(default=10)
    lora_r: int = Field(default=8)
    max_length: int = Field(default=512)

    def model_post_init(self, __context: Any) -> None:
        """Resolve base_model_name from registry when not explicitly set."""
        if not self.base_model_name:
            from model_training.model_configs import ModelRegistry

            mc = ModelRegistry.default().get(self.model_config_name)
            object.__setattr__(self, "base_model_name", mc.model_id)

    @field_validator("lr")
    @classmethod
    def _validate_lr(cls, v: float) -> float:
        """Ensure learning rate is strictly positive."""
        if v <= 0:
            raise ValueError(f"lr must be > 0, got {v}")
        return v

    @field_validator("alpha")
    @classmethod
    def _validate_alpha(cls, v: float) -> float:
        """Ensure alpha is in [0, 1]."""
        if not (0.0 <= v <= 1.0):
            raise ValueError(f"alpha must be in [0, 1], got {v}")
        return v

    @field_validator("temperature")
    @classmethod
    def _validate_temperature(cls, v: float) -> float:
        """Ensure temperature is strictly positive."""
        if v <= 0:
            raise ValueError(f"temperature must be > 0, got {v}")
        return v

    @field_validator("num_steps")
    @classmethod
    def _validate_num_steps(cls, v: int) -> int:
        """Ensure num_steps is strictly positive."""
        if v <= 0:
            raise ValueError(f"num_steps must be > 0, got {v}")
        return v


def _compute_kl_ce_loss(
    student_logits: Any,
    teacher_logits: Any,
    answer_start: int,
    config: D2LTrainConfig,
) -> tuple[Any, dict[str, float]]:
    """Compute blended KL-divergence and cross-entropy loss over the answer span.

    Accounts for the causal LM shift: logits at position ``k`` predict token
    ``k+1``, so we slice starting at ``answer_start - 1`` (clamped to 0).
    Returns a zero-loss tensor when the answer span is empty (e.g. truncation
    pushed ``answer_start`` beyond the sequence length).

    Args:
        student_logits: Student model logits of shape (batch, seq_len, vocab).
        teacher_logits: Teacher model logits of shape (batch, seq_len, vocab).
        answer_start: Token index where the answer span begins.  Tokens before
            this index are excluded from loss computation.
        config: Training configuration supplying alpha and temperature.

    Returns:
        A tuple of:
        - total_loss: Blended scalar tensor ``alpha * kl + (1 - alpha) * ce``.
        - metrics: Dict with keys ``kl_loss``, ``ce_loss``, ``total_loss``
          (Python floats, suitable for MLflow logging).
    """
    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
    import torch.nn.functional as functional  # noqa: PLC0415

    alpha = config.alpha
    temp = config.temperature

    # Guard against empty answer span (truncation pushed answer beyond seq_len).
    # answer_start >= seq_len means no answer tokens exist in the sequence.
    seq_len = student_logits.shape[1]
    if answer_start >= seq_len:
        logger.warning(
            "Answer span empty: answer_start=%d >= seq_len=%d. Returning zero loss.",
            answer_start,
            seq_len,
        )
        zero = torch.tensor(0.0, device=student_logits.device, requires_grad=True)
        return zero, {"kl_loss": 0.0, "ce_loss": 0.0, "total_loss": 0.0}

    # Causal LM shift: logits at position k predict token k+1, so the logit
    # that predicts the first answer token is at position answer_start - 1.
    logit_start = max(0, answer_start - 1)

    # Slice to answer span only (shift-aware)
    s = student_logits[:, logit_start:, :]
    t = teacher_logits[:, logit_start:, :]

    vocab = s.shape[-1]

    # KL divergence: temperature-scaled, batchmean reduction
    kl = functional.kl_div(
        functional.log_softmax(s / temp, dim=-1),
        functional.softmax(t / temp, dim=-1),
        reduction="batchmean",
    )

    # CE: student predictions vs teacher hard labels
    ce = functional.cross_entropy(s.reshape(-1, vocab), t.argmax(-1).reshape(-1))

    # Blended loss
    total = alpha * kl + (1 - alpha) * ce

    return total, {
        "kl_loss": kl.item(),
        "ce_loss": ce.item(),
        "total_loss": total.item(),
    }


def _training_step(
    record: dict[str, Any],
    base_model: Any,
    tokenizer: Any,
    hypernet: Any,
    hc: Any,
    config: D2LTrainConfig,
) -> tuple[Any, dict[str, float]]:
    """Execute a single training step with two-pass teacher/student separation.

    Pass 1 extracts activations from activation_text only (no answer tokens).
    Pass 2 runs teacher (no_grad) and student (with LoRA) on teacher_text.

    Args:
        record: Data record with 'activation_text' and 'teacher_text' fields.
        base_model: Base LM in eval mode.
        tokenizer: Tokenizer matching base_model.
        hypernet: HyperLoRA in train mode.
        hc: HypernetConfig with layer_indices and lora_config.
        config: Training configuration.

    Returns:
        Tuple of (loss_tensor, metrics_dict).
    """
    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415

    from model_training.d2l_lora import apply_functional_lora  # noqa: PLC0415
    from model_training.d2l_probe import extract_activations_with_model  # noqa: PLC0415

    # Pass 1: extract activations from activation_text (context only, no answer tokens)
    features, attn_mask = extract_activations_with_model(
        text=record["activation_text"],
        model=base_model,
        tokenizer=tokenizer,
        layer_indices=list(hc.layer_indices),
        max_length=config.max_length,
    )

    # Hypernetwork forward — OUTSIDE torch.no_grad to preserve autograd graph
    lora_dict, _ = hypernet.generate_weights(features, attn_mask, None)

    # Compute answer_start: token offset where answer begins in teacher_text
    answer_start = len(
        tokenizer(
            record["activation_text"],
            truncation=True,
            max_length=config.max_length,
        )["input_ids"]
    )

    # Pass 2 teacher: run base model under no_grad to get teacher logits
    teacher_inputs = tokenizer(
        record["teacher_text"],
        return_tensors="pt",
        truncation=True,
        max_length=config.max_length,
    )
    try:
        device = next(base_model.parameters()).device
    except StopIteration:
        device = torch.device("cpu")
    teacher_inputs = {k: v.to(device) for k, v in teacher_inputs.items()}

    with torch.no_grad():
        teacher_out = base_model(**teacher_inputs, output_hidden_states=False)
    teacher_logits = teacher_out.logits

    # Pass 2 student: run base model with functional LoRA patches
    with apply_functional_lora(base_model, lora_dict, hc):
        student_out = base_model(**teacher_inputs, output_hidden_states=False)
    student_logits = student_out.logits

    return _compute_kl_ce_loss(student_logits, teacher_logits, answer_start, config)


def _save_checkpoint(
    step: int,
    hypernet: Any,
    optimizer: Any,
    scheduler: Any,
    config: D2LTrainConfig,
    hc: Any,
    best_loss: float,
    full: bool = False,
) -> Path:
    """Save a tiered checkpoint to disk.

    Lightweight checkpoint: hypernet weights, config, step, layer indices, best_loss.
    Full checkpoint: additionally includes optimizer state, scheduler state, RNG state.

    Args:
        step: Current training step number.
        hypernet: HyperLoRA model.
        optimizer: AdamW optimizer.
        scheduler: LR scheduler.
        config: Training configuration.
        hc: HypernetConfig with attention_layer_indices.
        best_loss: Best loss seen so far.
        full: If True, save full checkpoint with optimizer/scheduler/RNG state.

    Returns:
        Path to the saved checkpoint file.
    """
    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415

    ckpt_dir = Path(config.checkpoint_dir)
    ckpt_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    payload: dict[str, Any] = {
        "hypernet_state_dict": hypernet.state_dict(),
        "config_json": config.model_dump(),
        "step": step,
        "attention_layer_indices": list(hc.layer_indices),
        "best_loss": best_loss,
    }

    if full:
        payload["optimizer_state_dict"] = optimizer.state_dict()
        payload["scheduler_state_dict"] = scheduler.state_dict()
        payload["rng_state"] = torch.get_rng_state()
        if torch.cuda.is_available():
            payload["cuda_rng_state"] = torch.cuda.get_rng_state()

    ckpt_path = ckpt_dir / f"ckpt-{step}.pt"
    torch.save(payload, ckpt_path)
    logger.info("Checkpoint saved: %s (full=%s)", ckpt_path, full)
    return ckpt_path


def _setup_mlflow(config: D2LTrainConfig) -> None:
    """Configure MLflow tracking URI and experiment name.

    Delegates to training_common.setup_mlflow. The d2l training loop always
    calls mlflow.start_run() directly after this, so we do not gate on
    RUNE_DISABLE_MLFLOW here — that gate lives in setup_mlflow itself but the
    existing callers in train_d2l_qwen3 call mlflow directly after this
    function returns, so we preserve the unconditional call behaviour.

    Args:
        config: Training configuration supplying experiment_name.
    """
    from model_training.training_common import setup_mlflow  # noqa: PLC0415

    setup_mlflow(
        config.experiment_name,
        os.environ.get("MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI"),
    )


def _dry_run_validate_shapes(config: D2LTrainConfig) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Validate tensor shapes with a single forward pass, no optimizer step.

    Loads the base model and hypernet, generates one needle record, runs one
    training step without backward/optimizer, asserts all shapes are correct,
    and returns a shape summary.

    Args:
        config: Training configuration.

    Returns:
        Dict with shape validation results: features, lora_dict, student/teacher logits.
    """
    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
    from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer  # noqa: PLC0415

    from model_training.d2l_config import build_hypernet_config  # noqa: PLC0415
    from model_training.d2l_data import generate_needle_dataset  # noqa: PLC0415
    from model_training.d2l_probe import (  # noqa: PLC0415
        extract_activations_with_model,
    )
    from model_training.sakana_d2l import (  # noqa: PLC0415
        get_aggregator_config,
        transfer_aggregator_weights,
    )

    # Guard: dry-run also requires probe cache to produce correct LoRA dimensions.
    _require_probe_cache(config.model_config_name)

    logger.info("Dry-run: loading tokenizer and base model...")
    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(config.base_model_name)
    base_model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(
        config.base_model_name,
        output_hidden_states=True,
    ).eval()

    logger.info("Dry-run: building hypernet config and transferring weights...")
    hc = build_hypernet_config(
        config.model_config_name,
        aggregator_config=get_aggregator_config(config.sakana_checkpoint_path),
        lora_r=config.lora_r,
    )

    from ctx_to_lora.modeling.hypernet import HyperLoRA  # noqa: PLC0415

    hypernet = HyperLoRA(hc).to(torch.float32)
    hypernet = transfer_aggregator_weights(hypernet, config.sakana_checkpoint_path)
    hypernet.eval()

    device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
    base_model = base_model.to(device)
    hypernet = hypernet.to(device)

    records = generate_needle_dataset(n=1)
    record = records[0]

    logger.info("Dry-run: running single forward pass for shape validation...")
    features, attn_mask = extract_activations_with_model(
        text=record["activation_text"],
        model=base_model,
        tokenizer=tokenizer,
        layer_indices=list(hc.layer_indices),
        max_length=config.max_length,
    )

    with torch.no_grad():
        lora_dict, _ = hypernet.generate_weights(features, attn_mask, None)

    tokenizer_out = tokenizer(
        record["teacher_text"],
        return_tensors="pt",
        truncation=True,
        max_length=config.max_length,
    )
    teacher_inputs = {k: v.to(device) for k, v in tokenizer_out.items()}

    with torch.no_grad():
        teacher_out = base_model(**teacher_inputs, output_hidden_states=False)
    teacher_logits = teacher_out.logits

    from model_training.d2l_lora import apply_functional_lora  # noqa: PLC0415

    with apply_functional_lora(base_model, lora_dict, hc):
        with torch.no_grad():
            student_out = base_model(**teacher_inputs, output_hidden_states=False)
    student_logits = student_out.logits

    # Assert shapes
    assert features.ndim == 4, f"features must be 4D, got {features.ndim}D"  # noqa: S101
    assert student_logits.shape == teacher_logits.shape, (  # noqa: S101
        f"student/teacher logit shapes must match: "
        f"{student_logits.shape} vs {teacher_logits.shape}"
    )

    shape_summary = {
        "features_shape": list(features.shape),
        "attn_mask_shape": list(attn_mask.shape),
        "lora_modules": list(lora_dict.keys()),
        "student_logits_shape": list(student_logits.shape),
        "teacher_logits_shape": list(teacher_logits.shape),
        "status": "OK",
    }

    for mod_name, ab in lora_dict.items():
        shape_summary[f"lora_{mod_name}_A_shape"] = list(ab["A"].shape)
        shape_summary[f"lora_{mod_name}_B_shape"] = list(ab["B"].shape)

    logger.info("Dry-run shape validation passed: %s", shape_summary)
    return shape_summary


def train_d2l_qwen3(config: D2LTrainConfig) -> dict[str, Any]:  # noqa: C901
    """Run KL-divergence context distillation training.

    Three execution modes controlled by config flags:
    - dry_run=True: Validate shapes with single forward pass, no optimizer step.
    - smoke_test=True: Run min(num_steps, 5) steps, assert finite decreasing loss.
    - default: Full training from dataset with checkpointing and MLflow tracking.

    Args:
        config: Training configuration.

    Returns:
        Dictionary with training results:
            - final_loss: Loss at the last step.
            - best_loss: Lowest loss seen during training.
            - num_steps_completed: Number of training steps completed.
            - checkpoint_dir: Path to checkpoint directory.
            - shape_summary (dry_run only): Tensor shape validation results.
    """
    import mlflow  # noqa: PLC0415
    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
    from ctx_to_lora.modeling.hypernet import HyperLoRA  # noqa: PLC0415
    from torch.nn.utils import clip_grad_norm_  # noqa: PLC0415
    from torch.optim import AdamW  # noqa: PLC0415
    from torch.optim.lr_scheduler import (  # noqa: PLC0415
        CosineAnnealingLR,
        LinearLR,
        SequentialLR,
    )
    from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer  # noqa: PLC0415

    from model_training.d2l_config import build_hypernet_config  # noqa: PLC0415
    from model_training.d2l_data import (  # noqa: PLC0415
        generate_needle_dataset,
        load_jsonl,
        split_by_task_id,
    )
    from model_training.sakana_d2l import (  # noqa: PLC0415
        get_aggregator_config,
        transfer_aggregator_weights,
    )

    # Mode dispatch: dry_run exits after shape validation
    if config.dry_run:
        shape_summary = _dry_run_validate_shapes(config)
        return {"shape_summary": shape_summary, "status": "dry_run_complete"}

    # Smoke test caps steps at 5
    if config.smoke_test:
        config = config.model_copy(update={"num_steps": min(config.num_steps, 5)})

    # Guard: require probe cache when not in smoke_test mode.
    # smoke_test uses generate_needle_dataset and may not have a real probe cache.
    if not config.smoke_test:
        _require_probe_cache(config.model_config_name)

    num_steps = config.num_steps
    warmup_steps = config.warmup_steps

    # Load tokenizer and base model
    logger.info("Loading tokenizer: %s", config.base_model_name)
    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(config.base_model_name)

    logger.info("Loading base model: %s", config.base_model_name)
    base_model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(
        config.base_model_name,
        output_hidden_states=True,
    ).eval()

    # Build hypernet config with aggregator config from checkpoint
    logger.info(
        "Building hypernet config from checkpoint: %s",
        config.sakana_checkpoint_path,
    )
    hc = build_hypernet_config(
        config.model_config_name,
        aggregator_config=get_aggregator_config(config.sakana_checkpoint_path),
        lora_r=config.lora_r,
    )

    # Create hypernet and transfer aggregator weights (freezes aggregator)
    hypernet = HyperLoRA(hc).to(torch.float32)
    hypernet = transfer_aggregator_weights(hypernet, config.sakana_checkpoint_path)
    hypernet.train()

    # Device selection: cuda > mps > cpu
    from shared.hardware import get_best_device  # noqa: PLC0415

    device = torch.device(get_best_device())
    logger.info("Using device: %s", device)
    base_model = base_model.to(device)
    hypernet = hypernet.to(device)

    # Optimizer: only trainable params (head + projections, not frozen aggregator)
    trainable_params = [p for p in hypernet.parameters() if p.requires_grad]
    logger.info("Trainable params: %d", sum(p.numel() for p in trainable_params))
    optimizer = AdamW(trainable_params, lr=config.lr)

    # Scheduler: linear warmup → cosine annealing
    scheduler = SequentialLR(
        optimizer,
        schedulers=[
            LinearLR(optimizer, start_factor=0.01, total_iters=warmup_steps),
            CosineAnnealingLR(
                optimizer,
                T_max=max(1, num_steps - warmup_steps),
                eta_min=1e-6,
            ),
        ],
        milestones=[warmup_steps],
    )

    # Data loading
    if config.smoke_test or config.dataset_path is None:
        records = generate_needle_dataset(n=20)
        logger.info("Using needle dataset (%d records)", len(records))
    else:
        all_records = load_jsonl(config.dataset_path)
        records, _ = split_by_task_id(all_records)
        logger.info(
            "Loaded %d training records from %s",
            len(records),
            config.dataset_path,
        )

    if not records:
        raise ValueError("No training records loaded; cannot train on empty dataset.")

    # MLflow setup and training loop
    _setup_mlflow(config)

    best_loss = float("inf")
    final_loss = float("inf")
    step_losses: list[float] = []

    with mlflow.start_run(run_name=f"{config.experiment_name}-step{num_steps}"):
        mlflow.log_params(config.model_dump())

        for step in range(1, num_steps + 1):
            record = records[(step - 1) % len(records)]

            loss, metrics = _training_step(
                record=record,
                base_model=base_model,
                tokenizer=tokenizer,
                hypernet=hypernet,
                hc=hc,
                config=config,
            )

            loss.backward()
            clip_grad_norm_(trainable_params, config.grad_clip)
            optimizer.step()
            scheduler.step()
            optimizer.zero_grad()

            step_loss = metrics["total_loss"]
            step_losses.append(step_loss)
            final_loss = step_loss
            if step_loss < best_loss:
                best_loss = step_loss

            mlflow.log_metrics(metrics, step=step)
            logger.info(
                "Step %d/%d — loss=%.4f (kl=%.4f, ce=%.4f)",
                step,
                num_steps,
                metrics["total_loss"],
                metrics["kl_loss"],
                metrics["ce_loss"],
            )

            # Tiered checkpointing
            if step % config.full_checkpoint_every == 0:
                ckpt_path = _save_checkpoint(
                    step=step,
                    hypernet=hypernet,
                    optimizer=optimizer,
                    scheduler=scheduler,
                    config=config,
                    hc=hc,
                    best_loss=best_loss,
                    full=True,
                )
                mlflow.log_artifact(str(ckpt_path))
            elif step % config.checkpoint_every == 0:
                ckpt_path = _save_checkpoint(
                    step=step,
                    hypernet=hypernet,
                    optimizer=optimizer,
                    scheduler=scheduler,
                    config=config,
                    hc=hc,
                    best_loss=best_loss,
                    full=False,
                )
                mlflow.log_artifact(str(ckpt_path))

    # Smoke test assertions
    if config.smoke_test:
        for i, sl in enumerate(step_losses):
            assert torch.isfinite(torch.tensor(sl)), (  # noqa: S101
                f"Smoke test: loss at step {i + 1} is not finite: {sl}"
            )
        assert step_losses[-1] < step_losses[0], (  # noqa: S101
            f"Smoke test: final loss {step_losses[-1]:.4f} not less than "
            f"initial loss {step_losses[0]:.4f}"
        )
        assert any(p.grad is not None for p in trainable_params), (  # noqa: S101
            "Smoke test: no trainable param has non-None gradient after training"
        )

    return {
        "final_loss": final_loss,
        "best_loss": best_loss,
        "num_steps_completed": num_steps,
        "checkpoint_dir": config.checkpoint_dir,
    }


# Public alias so callers don't need to reference the private name.
compute_kl_ce_loss = _compute_kl_ce_loss

103	def _override_lora_alpha(
104	    model: Any, adapter_name: str, new_alpha: int
105	) -> None:
106	    """Re-scale a loaded LoRA adapter's effective alpha in place.
107	
108	    PEFT's ``LoraLayer`` caches ``scaling[adapter_name] = alpha / r`` at
109	    layer-construction time — mutating ``model.peft_config[adapter].lora_alpha``
110	    after ``PeftModel.from_pretrained`` does NOT propagate, so we walk
111	    the module tree and update each layer's cached scaling directly.
112	
113	    Also mirrors the change into ``peft_config`` so downstream code that
114	    inspects the config (e.g. saving the adapter) sees the new alpha.
115	    """
116	    if hasattr(model, "peft_config") and adapter_name in model.peft_config:
117	        # Keep config consistent with the module state for any downstream
118	        # serialization / inspection.
119	        model.peft_config[adapter_name].lora_alpha = new_alpha
120	
121	    updated = 0
122	    for module in model.modules():
123	        if not hasattr(module, "scaling") or not hasattr(module, "r"):
124	            continue
125	        scaling = getattr(module, "scaling", None)
126	        r_map = getattr(module, "r", None)
127	        if not isinstance(scaling, dict) or not isinstance(r_map, dict):
128	            continue
129	        if adapter_name not in scaling or adapter_name not in r_map:
130	            continue
131	        rank = r_map[adapter_name]
132	        if rank <= 0:
133	            continue
134	        scaling[adapter_name] = new_alpha / rank
135	        updated += 1
136	    logger.info(
137	        "LoRA alpha override: adapter=%s new_alpha=%d updated_layers=%d",
138	        adapter_name,
139	        new_alpha,
140	        updated,
141	    )
142	
143	
144	def _override_lora_dropout(
145	    model: Any, adapter_name: str, new_p: float
146	) -> None:
147	    """Override the dropout probability of a loaded LoRA adapter in place.
148	
149	    ``layer.lora_dropout`` is an ``nn.ModuleDict`` keyed by adapter name
150	    whose values are ``nn.Dropout`` modules. We update the ``.p``
151	    attribute on each such module so new mini-batches see the new rate.
152	
153	    Passing ``new_p == 0.0`` effectively disables LoRA dropout for the
154	    adapter without rewiring the module graph.
155	    """
156	    if not 0.0 <= new_p <= 1.0:
157	        raise ValueError(f"dropout probability must be in [0, 1], got {new_p}")
158	
159	    if hasattr(model, "peft_config") and adapter_name in model.peft_config:
160	        model.peft_config[adapter_name].lora_dropout = new_p
161	
162	    updated = 0
163	    for module in model.modules():
164	        drop_map = getattr(module, "lora_dropout", None)
165	        if drop_map is None:
166	            continue
167	        # lora_dropout is an nn.ModuleDict; tolerate plain dict too.
168	        if hasattr(drop_map, "__contains__") and adapter_name in drop_map:
169	            target = drop_map[adapter_name]
170	            if hasattr(target, "p"):
171	                target.p = float(new_p)
172	                updated += 1
173	    logger.info(
174	        "LoRA dropout override: adapter=%s new_p=%.3f updated_layers=%d",
175	        adapter_name,
176	        new_p,
177	        updated,
178	    )

183	) -> None:

185	
186	    Raised early (before any GPU imports) so callers get a fast, cheap
187	    signal on misconfiguration.

190	        raise ValueError(
191	            "train_qlora: pass dataset_path XOR session_id, not both"
192	        )
193	    if dataset_path is None and session_id is None:
194	        raise ValueError(
195	            "train_qlora: either dataset_path or session_id must be provided"
196	        )
197	    if encoding_mode not in ("multi_turn", "single_turn"):

204	def _build_training_dataset(
205	    *,
206	    dataset_cls: Any,
207	    session_id: str | None,
208	    dataset_path: str | None,
209	    encoding_mode: str,
210	    diff_aware_loss: bool = False,
211	) -> Any:
212	    """Build an SFT ``Dataset`` from either a mined-pairs JSONL or a trajectory.
213	
214	    ``dataset_cls`` is the ``datasets.Dataset`` class injected by the caller
215	    so this helper stays GPU-import-free at module level while still producing
216	    a real ``datasets.Dataset`` at call time.
217	
218	    When ``diff_aware_loss=True`` and ``dataset_path`` is set, ``pre_code`` and
219	    ``post_code`` columns are attached alongside ``messages`` so the
220	    :class:`~model_training.diff_loss.DiffWeightedDataCollator` hunk path can
221	    compute line-level diff weights.  Trajectory-sourced datasets do not carry
222	    pre/post context, so the collator will log-warn-once and fall back to the
223	    legacy set-based path.
224	    """
225	    from typing import Literal, cast  # noqa: PLC0415
226	
227	    from model_training.d2l_data import (  # type: ignore[attr-defined]  # noqa: PLC0415
228	        load_jsonl,  # noqa: PLC0415
229	        pairs_to_chat_messages,
230	    )
231	    from model_training.trajectory import (  # noqa: PLC0415
232	        format_for_sft,
233	        load_trajectory,
234	    )
235	
236	    if dataset_path is not None:
237	        pairs = load_jsonl(dataset_path)
238	        mode = cast(Literal["multi_turn", "single_turn"], encoding_mode)
239	        conversations, pre_post = pairs_to_chat_messages(pairs, mode=mode)
240	        if not conversations:
241	            raise ValueError(
242	                f"dataset_path {dataset_path} produced no SFT conversations"
243	            )
244	        if diff_aware_loss:
245	            return dataset_cls.from_list(
246	                [
247	                    {
248	                        "messages": c,
249	                        "pre_code": pp["pre_code"],
250	                        "post_code": pp["post_code"],
251	                    }
252	                    for c, pp in zip(conversations, pre_post)
253	                ]
254	            )
255	        return dataset_cls.from_list([{"messages": c} for c in conversations])
256	
257	    # session_id is not None at this point (validated above).
258	    trajectory = load_trajectory(str(session_id))
259	    messages = format_for_sft(trajectory)
260	    if not messages:
261	        raise ValueError(
262	            f"Trajectory {session_id} is not successful or has no SFT messages"
263	        )
264	    return dataset_cls.from_list([{"messages": messages}])

283	    ``DiffAwareSFTTrainer`` and wraps its auto-built collator with
284	    :class:`~model_training.diff_loss.DiffWeightedDataCollator` so each
285	    batch carries a ``loss_weights`` tensor.

312	def _build_sft_config(
313	    *,
314	    sft_config_cls: Any,
315	    output_dir: str,
316	    resolved_epochs: int,
317	    learning_rate: float,
318	    warmup_ratio: float | None,
319	    resolved_lr_sched: str,

321	    report_to: str,

326	
327	    Extracted to keep ``train_qlora`` under the C901 complexity limit.
328	    ``neftune_noise_alpha`` is only passed when not None so SFTConfig's own
329	    default (feature disabled) applies when the caller omits it.
330	    """
331	    kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {
332	        "output_dir": output_dir,
333	        "num_train_epochs": resolved_epochs,
334	        "learning_rate": learning_rate,

336	        "lr_scheduler_type": resolved_lr_sched,
337	        "bf16": True,
338	        "per_device_train_batch_size": 1,
339	        "gradient_accumulation_steps": resolved_grad_accum,
340	        "save_strategy": "no",
341	        "logging_steps": 1,
342	        "report_to": report_to,
343	        "eval_strategy": "no",
344	        "assistant_only_loss": not diff_aware_loss,
345	    }
346	    if neftune_noise_alpha is not None:

349	        # TRL strips unknown columns by default; keep pre_code / post_code

352	    return sft_config_cls(**kwargs)
